CREATE TABLE test_list (id INT, values LIST<INT>);
SELECT array_push_front(3, values) FROM test_list WHERE id = 1;
SELECT array_push_front(7, array_push_front(3, values)) FROM test_list WHERE id = 2;
SELECT id, array_push_front(0, values) as new_list FROM test_list;
WITH prepended AS (SELECT id, array_push_front(9, values) as new_values FROM test_list) SELECT * FROM prepended WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, (SELECT array_push_front(2, values) FROM test_list WHERE id = 3) as new_values FROM test_list WHERE id != 3;
SELECT array_push_front(5, (SELECT values FROM test_list WHERE id = 2)) as result;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (id INT, values LIST<INT>);
SELECT t1.id, array_push_front(t2.id, t1.values) as new_list FROM test_list t1 JOIN additional_data t2 ON t1.id = t2.id;
UPDATE test_list SET values = array_push_front(1, values) WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, array_push_front(-1, array_push_front(-99, values)) as nested_prepended FROM test_list;
